name: SEMrush API
what_it_does: Provides programmatic access to SEMrush's SEO database for keyword rankings, organic traffic estimates, backlink profiles, competitor keyword gaps, and SERP data across 140+ countries.
use_cases: Fetch keyword difficulty and search volume data to prioritize content production queues
Pull competitor organic keyword lists to identify content gap opportunities
Monitor daily rank changes for a target domain's keyword portfolio
Retrieve backlink profiles to audit link-building campaigns
Automate domain traffic and authority metrics for client SEO reporting dashboards
not_for: Real-time SERP scraping (data is cached and updated periodically, not live)
Small-budget projects — API unit costs add up quickly for high-volume queries
Paid advertising creative or bidding automation (use Google Ads API instead)
best_when: An agent needs to enrich content briefs or competitive analyses with keyword volume, difficulty, and traffic estimates at scale without manual SEMrush UI interaction.
avoid_when: Query volume will exceed plan unit limits daily or the use case requires real-time SERP data fresher than SEMrush's update cycle.

auth.notes: Single API key passed as a query parameter (`key=`). No OAuth or token refresh needed. Keys are scoped per SEMrush account; all endpoints share one key.

pricing.paid_tiers: API access is included in Pro ($139.95/mo, ~10 API units/day), Guru ($249.95/mo, more units), and Business ($499.95/mo) plans; additional unit packs purchasable.
pricing.notes: Units are the billing currency — each API request costs a variable number of units depending on endpoint and rows returned. Unit budgets reset daily.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits vary by plan; typically 10 requests/second per API key. Daily unit quotas are the primary constraint (10–3000+ units/day by plan).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors returned as plain-text lines (not JSON) with a numeric code and message. Agents must parse non-standard error format; no structured error body.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only GET requests; safe to retry without side effects.
known_agent_gotchas: Responses default to `|`-delimited plain text, not JSON — agents must request `export_format=json` explicitly on supported endpoints
Unit cost per request is not returned in the response; agents cannot track spend without external accounting
Some endpoints return `ERROR 50 :: NOTHING FOUND` as a 200 OK, requiring content inspection to detect empty results
Daily unit quotas reset at midnight UTC, not the account's local timezone — scheduling matters for high-volume agents
Database selection (`database=us` etc.) silently affects result relevance; defaulting to 'us' for non-US domains returns misleading data

security_score_components.security_notes: API key is passed as a URL query parameter, which risks exposure in server logs and browser history. No IP allowlisting or key rotation mechanism documented.
